name: Umayya ibn Sa'd
title: Braves of Tayy
shortDescription: A Shia and warrior of the Tayy tribe in Kufa who reached Karbala on the eighth night of Muharram and was martyred in the first attack.
fullStory:
Umayya ibn Sa'd al-Ta'i was a sincere and renowned Shia of Kufa, belonging to the prominent Tayy tribe. He was known among the Kufans for his bravery and combat skills, and had always been a loyal follower of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS).

Hearing the news of Imam Hussain's (AS) presence in Karbala, Umayya could not sit idly in Kufa. Despite the strict surveillance by Ibn Ziyad's forces, he managed to leave Kufa at night and reached Imam Hussain's (AS) camp on the eighth night of Muharram.

On the morning of Ashura, when Umar Sa'd ordered the archers to fire and the all-out attack (the first attack) began, Umayya ibn Sa'd drew his sword and manfully charged into the heart of the enemy.

Possessing exemplary courage, he stood against the flood of enemies and, after a fierce battle, attained the great honor of martyrdom in that very first attack.
quoteArabic: السَّلامُ عَلى أُمَيَّةَ بْنِ سَعْدٍ الطّائِيِّ...
quote: (From Ziyarat Nahiya:) Peace be upon Umayya ibn Sa'd al-Ta'i.
